frameworks.name: Bayesian reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value updates weighted by inferred probability that outcomes come from hidden agents

frameworks.name: causal inference
frameworks.one_line: learners attribute outcomes to chosen option vs. external hidden cause

frameworks.name: one learning rate reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value updated from outcomes alone without causal attribution

frameworks.name: developmental cognitive neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: compares learning computations across childhood, adolescence, and adulthood

core_question: Do beliefs about external hidden causes shape reinforcement learning similarly across development from childhood to adulthood?
key_assumptions: outcomes can be attributed to choice or to hidden external agent
controllability beliefs modulate the magnitude of value updates
Bayesian RL formalizes causal attribution during learning
manipulations: valence of hidden-agent intervention (positive, negative, random)
two options with fixed reward probabilities
key_findings: explicit beliefs about hidden-agent intervention tracked true intervention probabilities across ages
adults' and adolescents' choices best fit by Bayesian RL with hidden-agent beliefs
children's choices best fit by a one learning rate model using outcomes alone
interpretations: children show explicit causal knowledge but do not implicitly use it to guide value updating
rational integration of causal structure into reinforcement learning strengthens with age
